name: Tree Basics
description: A binary tree node connects to left and right children, creating a recursive structure.
exampleJava: class TreeNode { int val; TreeNode left, right; }
commonMistakes: Forgetting null child checks
Treating trees like arrays without traversal logic

name: Recursive Tree Traversal
description: Visit the current node before, between, or after recursive calls to children.
commonMistakes: Missing the base case root == null
Using the wrong visit order

name: Level Order Traversal
description: Use a queue to process the tree level by level from left to right.
commonMistakes: Trying to do true level order with plain recursion
Not snapshotting the level size before iterating a level

name: Tree Height
description: The height of a tree is 1 plus the maximum height of its children.
commonMistakes: Counting edges when the problem expects node count
Returning 1 instead of 0 for null in node-based height definitions

name: Tree Diameter
description: Track the longest path passing through each node while computing heights.
commonMistakes: Recomputing heights from scratch at every node
Confusing height with diameter

name: Balanced Tree Check
description: A tree is balanced if the left and right subtree heights differ by at most 1 at every node.
commonMistakes: Checking only the root balance
Doing O(n^2) repeated height calls

name: BST Search
description: Use BST ordering to move left or right instead of scanning the entire tree.
exampleJava: if (target < root.val) root = root.left; else root = root.right;
commonMistakes: Ignoring the ordering property and traversing both sides
Going the wrong direction for comparisons

name: BST Insert and Delete
description: Insert by descending to the correct null child, and delete by handling 0, 1, or 2 child cases.
commonMistakes: Losing a subtree when reconnecting nodes
Not replacing a two-child node correctly

name: Tree Views
description: Capture visible nodes from a direction using BFS or ordered DFS.
commonMistakes: Taking every node instead of one per level
Mixing left view with level order output

name: Lowest Common Ancestor
description: Return the split point where one target is found in each subtree, or the target node itself.
exampleJava: if (left != null && right != null) return root;
commonMistakes: Returning too early when only one side has a match
Not handling the case where root is one of the targets

name: Tree Construction
description: Rebuild the tree by using traversal order properties and subtree boundaries.
commonMistakes: Not splitting the inorder range correctly
Using linear search repeatedly instead of an index map

name: Serialize Tree
description: Encode tree structure explicitly so null children and branching are preserved.
commonMistakes: Omitting null markers
Producing an encoding that cannot be decoded uniquely
